text:
It was the year the sun decided to hang its head. The roses weren't blooming; the eagles had flown south; and you could hear the cicadas singing their sweet peace somewhere westward. I ventured out one morning clad only in my underwear. That’s when I saw it.

There, in a patch of weeds bordering my favorite patch of sky-line oaks, was an old owl perched, staring knowingly right into my eyes. I didn’t speak.

For hours I sat there listening. An apprentice owl hooted in the trees. The old owl hooted back. I didn't understand what they were saying—but then again, I don't understand most things.

That evening I dreamt I was chasing butterflies through an enchanted forest. I found a magic flower that sang. I woke crying. Maybe I needed an owl.

So I took my sock-off companion outside. Maybe someone knew what I wanted. A few hours passed. An owl hooted. Maybe
